Summary: CEBPA is a master regulator of myeloid differentiation with expression peaking in the granulocyte-monocyte progenitor stage and diminishing during terminal granulocyte differentiation. CEBPA acts as a positive transcriptional regulator during granulocytic differentiation. In APL cell lines NB4 and HT93 undergoing ATRA-induced neutrophil differentiation, CEBPA is required for upregulation of HK3 and KLF5 and CEBPA knockdown reduced HK3 and KLF5 mRNA. Ectopic CEBPA expression in HT93 induced HK3 and KLF5 mRNA. In K562 cells, activation of full-length CEBPA-p42 specifically activated KLF5 transcription. Truncated CEBPA-p30 and a p42 mutant that prevents E2F interaction did not activate KLF5. HK3 mRNA was not detectable in K562 cells.
References: Federzoni, E. A., Humbert, M., Torbett, B. E., Behre, G., Fey, M. F., & Tschan, M. P. (2014). CEBPA-dependent HK3 and KLF5 expression in primary AML and during AML differentiation. Sci Rep, 4, 4261. https://doi.org/10.1038/srep04261
